fields.descr: The practice is implemented across all activities/services/products/offices/ facilities to which it could apply.
The practice is implemented across roughly 50% OR MORE of the activities/services/products/offices/ facilities to which it could apply.
The practice is implemented across roughly 50% OR LESS of the activities/services/products/offices/ facilities to which it could apply.
The practice is implemented across negligible to no activities/services/products/offices/ facilities to which it could apply.
The practice is not applicable to the organization, or the organization has no influence or control over its implementation.

fields.title: Sustainability
fields.extra: {"visibility":["public"],"highlights":[{"includes":["formalized-esg-strategy"],"picture":"static/img/other.png","text":"formalized targets"}, {"includes":["report-internally","report-externally"],"picture":"static/img/data-metrics.png","text":"tracking sustainability metrics"}, {"includes":["energy-publicly-reported","ghg-emissions-publicly-reported","water-publicly-reported","waste-publicly-reported"],"picture":"static/img/lighting.png","text":"reporting data publicly"}, {"includes":["energy-3rd-party-verified","ghg-emissions-3rd-party-verified","water-3rd-party-verified","waste-3rd-party-verified"],"picture":"static/img/lighting.png","text":"data metrics third-party verified"}]}

fields.title: Core Environmental, Social and Governance Assessment

Has your company assigned formal responsibility for directing and overseeing ESG performance to top/executive management, and designatied a point person(s) responsible for implementing requirements and commitments.
Have formalized ESG strategy and targets that: Define a future vision of ESG performance, Are clear, actionable, and acheivable, Are resourced effectively, Address material issues for the business
Does your organization have an Employee Code of Conduct in place?
Does your organization have a Supplier Code of Conduct in place?
Report on the following KPIs related to sustainable procurement...

Report internally, including to top management, on strategy: key ESG issues (revealed by materiality analysis), risk and crisis mitigation, progress toward goals, and plans/needs to address shortfalls.
Report externally on strategy: program elements, key ESG issues and goals, challenges, and continual improvement efforts in accordance with a reputable reporting standard (e.g. GRI, SASB).
Have ESG strategy and reports, targets, and KPIs periodically validated by an independent third party.
Has your organization had any significant ESG fines/ penalties (e.g. > USD $10,000, class action lawsuits, repeated breaches of Codes of Conduct) in the past five years?
Where is information about these incidents publicly available?
Conduct a systematic, rigorous assessment to identify and prioritize material ESG impacts, issues and opportunities.
Have job-specific responsibilities for implementing controls, etc. actively in place from management to applicable individual contributors.

Implement an environmental management system that conforms with a recognized international (e.g. ISO 14001) or local standard and indicate the coverage of the standard in your business and or facilities.
Have the system certified to a recognized local or international standard.
Have the system periodically audited internally and by an independent third party.

Is there any conflict minerals (tin, tantalum, tungsten, or gold) remaining in the product that your company manufactures, subcontracts, or sells? If so, has your company published a due diligence report or formal policy on conflict minerals?
Is your company sourcing from any high risk regions (e.g. DRC, Xinjiang, etc.)?
Has your company implemented a monitoring and investigation system to ensure conformity with local laws, ILO requirements, and your Codes of Conduct?
Does your company report Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) on Employee Health & Safety?
Does your company report Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) on Working Conditions?
Does your company report Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) on Career Management & Training?
Does your company report Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) on Diversity, Discrimination & Harassment?
Does your company report Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) on Recordable Incident Rate?
Does your company report Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) on Lost-time Case Rate?
Does your company report Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) on Days Away, Restricted, and Transfer (DART) Rate?
Does your company report Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) on Work-related Fatalities?
GHG Emissions Data Third Party Verified
Adjust air/fuel ratio
fields.text:
Background

Some manufacturing processes may involve heating operations.

fields.title: Has your company assigned formal responsibility for directing and overseeing ESG performance to top/executive management, and designatied a point person(s) responsible for implementing requirements and commitments.

Report internally, including to top management, on strategy: key ESG issues (revealed by materiality analysis), risk and crisis mitigation, progress toward goals, and plans/needs to address shortfalls.
